message:
feat(ffs-cli): emit raw hex for btrfs chunk dump

build_dump_group_output's btrfs branch was returning a placeholder
limitation ("raw hex for btrfs chunk dump is not currently available")
whenever the user passed --hex on a btrfs filesystem. The structured
chunk view (DumpGroupOutput.btrfs_chunk) was always populated, but
the raw_hex field stayed None and the user had to either drop into
the raw block dump path or read the chunk back through a separate
tool to see the on-disk bytes.

Implement encode_btrfs_chunk_entry_bytes to serialize a
ffs_ondisk::BtrfsChunkEntry back into its on-disk little-endian
form:

- Pre-size the Vec to 17 (key) + 48 (chunk header) +
  num_stripes * 32 (per-stripe devid+offset+uuid) using
  saturating_mul so a pathological num_stripes can't panic during
  capacity computation.
- debug_assert_eq the declared num_stripes against stripes.len() so
  a corrupted descriptor surfaces in debug builds rather than
  silently emitting truncated bytes.
- Emit fields in the exact on-disk order: key.objectid,
  key.item_type, key.offset, length, owner, stripe_len, chunk_type,
  io_align, io_width, sector_size, num_stripes, sub_stripes, then
  each stripe's devid, offset, dev_uuid.
- All multi-byte integers via to_le_bytes() to match the on-disk
  endianness contract; dev_uuid is already a fixed [u8; 16].

Wire it into build_dump_group_output's btrfs match arm: when --hex
is requested, look up the chunk by index from
open_fs.btrfs_context().chunks, encode it, and run it through the
existing bytes_to_hex_dump formatter so the output matches the
formatting used by the descriptor / extent2 dump paths. Drop the
"not currently available" limitations push.

Update the existing dump_group btrfs test:
- Replace `assert!(output.raw_hex.is_none())` with an unwrap on
  raw_hex and assert it starts with the expected first hex line
  ("00000000: 00 01 00 00 00 00 00 00 e4 00 00 00 00 00 00 00\n"
  for the test fixture's chunk).
- Replace the `limitations.contains("raw hex for btrfs chunk dump")`
  assertion with `output.limitations.is_empty()` since that
  limitation is no longer emitted.

Beads-only update on bd-4h62v (unrelated ScrubDaemon mount
lifecycle issue) is split into a separate commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
